primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivable for IFRS hedge accounting for foreign currency risk

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs define hedged items, restrict hedged items to external parties, provide an exception for intragroup monetary items, and specify fair value hedge accounting.
This paragraph requires recognition of exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in profit or loss (or OCI), creating foreign currency exposure.

supporting_authority.reason: This paragraph sets out the conditions for hedge accounting, including formal documentation and effectiveness assessment.
These paragraphs specify the accounting treatment for a fair value hedge.
These paragraphs specify the accounting treatment for a cash flow hedge.
These paragraphs provide guidance on translation of foreign operations and treatment of exchange differences.

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosures about hedge accounting are not relevant to the eligibility question.
Disclosures about interests in subsidiaries are not directly relevant.
Presentation of financial instruments is not relevant.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items
No hedge accounting – recognition of FX differences in profit or loss

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of foreign currency risk on intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits designation of the foreign currency risk of a monetary intragroup asset as the hedged item in a fair value hedge, provided the criteria (documentation, effectiveness) are met and the FX differences are not eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of foreign currency risk on intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also allows a cash flow hedge designation for the foreign currency risk affecting the cash flows of a monetary asset, if the entity elects to hedge the variability in functional currency cash flows.

approaches.label: Recognition of foreign exchange differences in profit or loss (or OCI)
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If the entity does not designate the exposure as a hedged item, the foreign exchange differences on the intragroup dividend receivable are recognised in profit or loss (or OCI) in accordance with IAS 21.
